Report file paths with line numbers where relevant. Don't edit anything.

    fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id [ddx-05b4cc9d]
    
    GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID) compared the GraphQL project id argument
    directly to WorkerRecord.ProjectRoot (a filesystem path), so every worker
    was filtered out whenever a non-empty id was passed. The per-project
    Workers view on the project home page was therefore always empty even
    when a Drain-queue dispatch had started a worker.
    
    Resolve the id to its canonical project path via ServerState.GetProjectByID
    before filtering, matching the approach already used by GetBeadSnapshots.
    An unknown project id returns an empty list (not an error), consistent
    with the prior behavior for non-matching filters.
    
    Chose Option 1 (id->path resolution at the filter site) over Option 2
    (store project id on WorkerRecord): one-line change, no schema or
    migration, no coupling between WorkerManager and ServerState required.
    
    Tests:
    - TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new): two registered projects
      with distinct paths/ids, one worker each; asserts workersByProject
      returns only the matching worker per project id, unknown id returns
      empty, and the global workers query still returns both.
    - TestGraphQLWorkers (updated): now queries by the registered project
      id instead of the working-directory path, so the assertion actually
      exercises the resolver rather than relying on the bug.
    
    Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
    acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly.

# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills

feat(workers): persistent drain indicator + count-control UX [ddx-b6cf025c]

Adds a global drain-queue indicator in NavShell and an N-worker count
control on the workers overview so operators can see queue + worker
state from any route and scale drain capacity without picking a harness
each time.

- GraphQL: new queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) query backing the nav
  badge; returns zeros (not an error) for unknown projects.
- NavShell: DrainIndicator component polls every 3s, shows
  "Queue: X ready" when idle and "N workers · X ready" with a spinner
  when a drain is running. Clicking navigates to the workers overview.
- Workers overview: prominent "Drain workers: N" header with
  "+ Add worker" (one-click default-spec dispatch) and
  "− Remove worker" (confirm-gated stop of the oldest running drain
  worker). Co-exists with the existing per-harness Start/Stop picker.
- Config: new workers.default_spec and workers.max_count under
  .ddx/config.yaml. default_spec populates the + Add worker dispatch;
  max_count is enforced server-side so the button refuses past the cap.
- Tests: GraphQL resolver tests (known + unknown project); server tests
  for max_count enforcement, default_spec propagation, and the
  running-drain-worker filter; Playwright test covering the count panel,
  indicator visibility, + / - behaviour, and cross-route persistence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

Committed `04740d3` with 14 files, 1024 insertions. Changes:

- **GraphQL**: `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId)` query + resolver, returns zeros for unknown projects. Regenerated `generated.go` / `models.go`.
- **Config**: `.ddx/config.yaml` gains `workers.default_spec` (harness/profile/effort/min_tier/max_tier) and `workers.max_count`; schema JSON and Go types updated.
- **Dispatch adapter**: reads config on each `workerDispatch(kind: execute-loop)`, applies defaults, and refuses past `max_count`.
- **NavShell**: new `DrainIndicator.svelte` shows idle dot / spinner on every project route, polls the summary every 3s, links to the workers overview.
- **Workers overview**: new top panel "Drain workers: N" with `+ Add worker` (default-spec one-click) and `− Remove worker` (confirm-gated stop of oldest running drain). Coexists with the per-harness Start/Stop row controls.
- **Tests**: resolver tests (known + unknown project), adapter tests (max_count cap, default_spec propagation, kind/state/project filter), Playwright flow covering indicator, count control, and cross-route persistence.

All pre-commit hooks green (go-fmt, go-build, go-lint, go-test with `-race`).
